meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: SaaS Pricing Strategy: Solve 'No Project Ideas' for Students
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with project ideas. Learn how to build a SaaS pricing engine using Antigravity and Claude Code in this expert-led student case study.
content.answer_block: Overcoming 'no project ideas' starts by solving a specific business problem: SaaS pricing. By building a dynamic pricing engine, you learn database design, API integration, and user psychology. This project transforms a blank screen into a portfolio-ready SaaS feature, providing the technical foundation to scale into complex product development.
content.intro:
The blank editor is the student’s greatest adversary. You have the skills, you have the hunger, but you lack the north star—that one project idea that bridges the gap between 'tutorial coder' and 'software engineer.' Most students burn out trying to build the next Facebook, failing to realize that the most valuable SaaS products are built on boring, essential infrastructure.

The secret is not to invent a new world, but to master the mechanics of value. By building a sophisticated SaaS pricing engine, you aren't just coding a UI; you are architecting a revenue model. This case study turns the 'no project ideas' paralysis into a tactical exercise in logic, user flow, and monetization.

steps.title: Define the Tiered Architecture
steps.description: Before writing code, map out your pricing tiers (Free, Pro, Enterprise). This establishes your data schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a database schema for a SaaS pricing system with tiers, features, and recurring billing cycles.
steps.claude_code_command: /ask Define the PostgreSQL schema for a multi-tenant SaaS pricing model with table relationships.

steps.title: Build the Pricing Service
steps.description: Construct the logic to evaluate user plans against feature access. This is the core 'business logic' layer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript service class that validates if a user has access to a specific feature based on their current subscription tier.
steps.claude_code_command: /code Generate a FeatureGuard class that checks user subscription status against an array of feature flags.

steps.title: Integrate Stripe Checkout
steps.description: Connect your logic to a payment processor. This turns a prototype into a functional SaaS application.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Stripe webhook handler in Node.js to update user subscription status in the database upon successful checkout.
steps.claude_code_command: /ask How do I securely implement a Stripe webhook listener using Express and Typescript?

steps.title: Develop the Pricing UI
steps.description: Create a high-converting pricing table using React and Tailwind CSS that highlights the 'Pro' plan.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive React component for a 3-tier pricing table with a 'Most Popular' highlight using Tailwind.
steps.claude_code_command: /code Generate a PricingTable component with interactive toggle for monthly/yearly billing.

steps.title: Implement Analytics Hooks
steps.description: Track conversion events to understand user behavior, a key metric for any real-world SaaS.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Add basic analytics tracking for pricing page views and 'Start Trial' button clicks.
steps.claude_code_command: /ask Add PostHog event tracking to the pricing button click handlers.

pro_tips: Always build the pricing engine as a standalone module so it can be ported to your future projects.
Use 'Value-Based Pricing' labels instead of just feature lists to make your UI look like professional SaaS.
Implement a 'Grace Period' logic in your backend to handle failed credit card payments without locking users out immediately.
Always default to yearly billing in your UI design; it is the single biggest lever for increasing SaaS cash flow.
Build a 'Hidden' tier for enterprise clients to practice creating custom database records outside the standard flow.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is live, integrate an Auth provider like Clerk or Supabase to tie user identity to their specific pricing tier.
content.conversion_block: You now have the blueprint to turn a simple pricing page into a full-scale SaaS engine. Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start building the one that validates your skills to future employers. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
